name: Ray Noble
description: English jazz and big band musician (1903–1978)
abstract: Raymond Stanley Noble (17 December 1903 – 3 April 1978) was an English jazz and big band musician, who was a bandleader, composer and arranger, as well as a radio host, television and film comedian and actor; he also performed in the United States. He is best known for his signature tune, "The Very Thought of You", and for "Cherokee".

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee narrates a story about Charlie Parker ('Bird') and Teddy Reig dealing with a copyright issue regarding Ray Noble's composition 'Cherokee'. The interviewee (Jon Hendricks) does not mention any personal interaction with Ray Noble.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: And mostly like Benny Goodman, Paul Whiteman, Ray Noble—they had the hotel jobs, they were off about 11:30, 8:00 ‘til about 11:30 or 12. I remember one night Ray Noble came in and he told Basie he had a fresh tune that he wanted to record and it was “Cherokee.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Warren Vaché
about_quotes.evidence: I still can’t take the Beach Boys as seriously as I can take Louis Armstrong, you know, or for that matter even Ray Noble, guys that were serious musicians.
about_quotes.doc_title: Warren Vaché interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 23, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Arthur "Monk" Hazel
about_quotes.evidence: The next band he built was, uh, was Ray Noble's band when he came over here from England, which was the best band in the country at the time.

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Jerome
about_quotes.evidence: I didn’t know who Glenn was, never heard of him. You know he was a sideman then, as a matter of fact he was playing with Ray Noble
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Jerome interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: He said, 'You haven't heard anything until you hear Charlie Parker play 'Cherokee' -- you know, the Ray Noble composition.'
